name: Highbush Blueberry
summary: Native to the acidic upland soils of the Pocono Plateau — pH 4–5.5 is exactly what this site provides. More than 100 bird species consume the berries. White bell-shaped flowers in May are critical early nectar for native bees and bumble bees. Brilliant orange-red fall color.
notes: One of the best native shrubs for this exact site — the acidic, sandy, well-drained upland soils that many other shrubs struggle with are ideal for blueberry. Wire cage to protect from deer browse. Dioecious — plant at least two shrubs for fruit production. Spreads slowly by root suckers once established.
